description: Lock Chieftainship to formal hereditary chiefdom leadership and keep broad Social Stratification independent from the later chiefdom-specific office.

checks.reason: The corrected chronology uses the early-chiefdom literature's broad 7,000-years-ago lower bound rather than the previous unsupported -9000 date.
Broad social stratification is not allowed to depend on the later, narrower hereditary chiefdom office.
Kin and lineage organization enables inherited chiefly authority without making every band or lineage order a chiefdom.
Chiefly authority depends on controlling land, surplus, trade, and prestige goods, but property concepts are enabling rather than sufficient on their own.
